findings: xLSTM extends classic LSTM with two new cell variants: sLSTM (scalar memory, exponential gating, new memory mixing) and mLSTM (matrix memory, covariance update, fully parallelizable training)
Exponential gating addresses LSTM's inability to revise stored values when a more similar token appears later (the rare-token / nearest-neighbour-search failure mode)
mLSTM gains parallel training comparable to Transformer/Mamba while keeping recurrent constant-time inference

Reports favorable performance vs state-of-the-art Transformers AND Mamba at matched scale on both perplexity and scaling-law extrapolations — a third pole in the architecture comparison beyond just Mamba vs Transformer
Authored by Hochreiter (original LSTM author) and lab — a credibility signal for the recurrent revival narrative
limitations: Public reproductions of xLSTM scaling-law claims are still emerging; the paper's matched-compute comparison protocol differs slightly from Mamba's, complicating direct head-to-head numbers across the three architectures.
relevance: Important third architecture in the comparative space — expands the question from "Mamba vs Transformer" to "recurrent revival (Mamba, xLSTM, RWKV) vs Transformer".
